channel.title: Agentic Coding on Sojjwal Kelkar
channel.description: Recent content in Agentic Coding on Sojjwal Kelkar

item.title: If Rubens could delegate his paintings, why can't you delegate your code?
item.description:
I have previously argued that engineers using agentic coding should think like managers. But perhaps you are not interested in management. You don’t know what managers do and don’t want to learn to think like them. You are put off by all the manager-analogies and wish they’d stop. Perhaps you are the artistic type, who thinks of themselves as a software craftsperson.

So let’s change the subject and talk about something interesting, like art. Hey how about that Rubens? Do you know his process on some of his famous paintings like The Elevation of the Cross?

item.title: ICs Using AI Agents Should Think Like Managers
item.description:
Developers new to agentic coding are at risk of having the wrong expectations. When they try coding agents for the first time, it feels like magic. But cracks appear when AI is used without understanding its limitations. People tend to dismiss agentic coding if it doesn’t work “perfectly”. This is unfortunate, as coding agents are in fact very impressive.
It is said that managers have been vibe coding forever. Now, if ICs are also vibe coding, are they like managers?
